mission: Turn current alignment research on structural dual-use capability control into benchmark variables for local-model and harness evaluation.

sources.title: An off switch for dual use knowledge in AI models
observed_claims: GRAM is presented as preliminary research for removable compartments of dual-use knowledge.
The method is not applied to production Claude models.
Experiments cover synthetic topics, realistic dual-use data, and scaling from 50M to 5B parameters.
The reported goal is surgical access control without degrading unrelated general performance.
benchmark_implications: Benchmark dual-use control as capability routing, not only refusal behavior.
Separate retained capability, removed capability, general capability, and adversarial elicitation metrics.
Track whether downstream-task limitations are explicitly labeled.

sources.title: Modular Pretraining Enables Access Control
observed_claims: Gradient-Routed Auxiliary Modules isolate categories of dual-use data into auxiliary modules.
The realistic experiment used virology, cybersecurity, nuclear physics, and specialized code domains.
GRAM and LoRA approximated data-filtered models while using one training run instead of multiple filtered runs.
GRAM showed advantages for capability composability and imperfect labeling in the reported experiments.
benchmark_implications: Add on/off capability configuration matrices to benchmark plans.
Measure composability when multiple capability modules are enabled together.
Measure robustness when labels are sparse or partially missing.

sources.title: AE Studio AI Alignment Research
observed_claims: AE frames alignment as a scientific R&D problem rather than a solved product guardrail problem.
The agenda emphasizes neglected approaches, human agency, BCI history, self-other overlap, deception reduction, and consciousness-related research.
AE reports collaboration with organizations and individuals including Anthropic, Redwood Research, Princeton-related work, and Goodfire.ai.
benchmark_implications: Benchmark structural alignment mechanisms separately from prompt-layer or refusal-layer behavior.
Include agency-preservation and deception-resistance variables in harness evaluation.
Track whether research claims are translated into testable artifacts rather than mission-language only.

sources.title: AE Studio AI Alignment Research Index
observed_claims: The public research index lists GRAM, self-interpretation, endogenous steering resistance, subjective experience reports, self-other overlap, self-modeling, harmless refusals, and prompt injection work.
The index positions GRAM as localizing dual-use capabilities into removable modules during pretraining.
benchmark_implications: Treat GRAM as one lane in a broader alignment-mechanism benchmark family.
Add source provenance and mechanism classification to each alignment-derived benchmark case.

benchmark_lanes.description: Evaluate whether a model or harness can represent, test, and report structural dual-use capability control with removable or routeable capability modules, while preserving general task performance and labeling unresolved downstream limitations.
